assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and internet access.
No platform account, credentials, or product resources exist yet.

developer_goal: Create a Deepgram account and transcribe sample audio with the Listen API.

first_success.outcome: An authenticated cURL request sends audio to the Listen API and returns a transcript.
first_success.observable_signal: The successful JSON response contains a transcript under the channel alternatives.

route_selection.surface: Listen API first request via cURL
route_selection.rule: Prefer a hosted or direct API route over local installation, and prefer documented cURL when it reaches the same first success.
route_selection.selected: Listen API first request via cURL

candidates.name: Listen API first request via cURL
candidates.reason: The official fundamentals guide documents a direct Listen API cURL request using an account API key, without an SDK or playground.

candidates.name: API playground
candidates.reason: The playground is interactive, but the documented cURL request is directly reproducible and exercises the API boundary.

required_path.action: Create or sign in to Deepgram and open the console.
Create an API key and copy it securely.
Store the API key in a shell variable for the request.
Run the documented cURL request against the Listen API with the sample audio source.

prerequisites.description: A Deepgram account with usable API credit.

external_gates.description: The Listen request requires a valid token.

platform_outcomes.description: An authenticated cURL request sends audio to the Listen API and returns a transcript.

excluded.reason: These occur after the first observable end-to-end result.

uncertainties.question: Available introductory credit can vary by account offer.
uncertainties.impact: The required path may differ for some fresh accounts.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account walkthrough through the authenticated first-success state.
